name: Camino Lebaniego: Circular de Cabañes
name_original: Corral de los Moros – circular desde Cabañes
description: Jog the moderate 3.4-mile Corral de los Moros circular route from Cabañes, gaining 487 feet with panoramic views of the Picos de Europa.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Cillorigo De Liébana explore a mountainous region in Cantabria, Spain, largely encompassed by the Picos de Europa National Park. The terrain is shaped by the Deva River, which flows through the area, and features diverse forests and the impressive Gorge of La Hermida. This varied landscape offers a range of running experiences, from river valleys to high mountain paths. Best jogging routes around Cillorigo De Liébana The most popular jogging route is Sotres to Pico Boru Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This difficult route offers ascents into high mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Camino Lebaniego: Circular de Cabañes, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This route is part of the historic Camino Lebaniego, providing cultural insights and panoramic views. Local runners also love the Sotres Village – Refugio de la Terenosa loop from Sotres, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail leading through high mountain areas, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Jogging around Cillorigo De Liébana is defined by high mountain ranges, river valleys, and diverse woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Cillorigo De Liébana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Cillorigo De Liébana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cillorigo De Liébana?
A: There are over 30 running routes around Cillorigo De Liébana, offering a wide range of experiences. These include paths through river valleys, diverse woodlands, and challenging high mountain trails within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running paths suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options for less experienced runners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999026266/camino-lebaniego-circular-de-cabanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Lebaniego: Circular de Cabañes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.4-mile path that offers cultural insights and panoramic views without extreme difficulty. The region also offers short, gentle walks around villages like Tama.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Cillorigo De Liébana?
A: The terrain is incredibly varied, ranging from picturesque river valleys along the Deva River to dense forests like the Castañar de Pendes, and impressive high mountain landscapes within the Picos de Europa National Park. You'll find paths suitable for different fitness levels, from gentle slopes to significant ascents.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: Jogging routes often pass through stunning natural areas. You can explore the impressive Gorge of La Hermida, run alongside the Deva River, or discover ancient forests such as the Castañar de Pendes. Some routes also offer panoramic views of the Picos de Europa. For specific viewpoints, consider routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999026266/camino-lebaniego-circular-de-cabanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Lebaniego: Circular de Cabañes&lt;/a&gt; is part of the historic Camino Lebaniego pilgrimage route. You might also encounter charming villages like Colio or Salarzón, and historical sites such as the 10th-century church of Santa María de Lebeña.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Cillorigo De Liébana?
A: Cillorigo De Liébana benefits from a unique microclimate, making it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude routes within the Picos de Europa National Park, which provide cooler air and stunning views. Winter running is possible, but higher mountain trails may require specialized gear due to snow and ice.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999026266/camino-lebaniego-circular-de-cabanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Lebaniego: Circular de Cabañes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 miles long. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotres Village – Refugio de la Terenosa loop from Sotres&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.1-mile trail through high mountain areas.

Q: Can I find routes that are around 10 kilometers (6 miles) in length?
A: Yes, several routes are close to or exceed this distance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1228653998/sotres-to-pico-boru-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotres to Pico Boru Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 5.7-mile (9.1 km) route, offering significant ascents. For a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotres Village – Sotres loop from Sotres&lt;/a&gt; is 19.4 km (12 miles).

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Cillorigo De Liébana?
A: The running routes in Cillorigo De Liébana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning natural beauty, the varied terrain, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents into the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges along the longer mountain running trails?
A: Yes, for those tackling longer or more challenging mountain runs, there are huts and refuges available. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/896682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jou de los Cabrones Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/175952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Urriellu Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/890762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Terenosa Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. These can be crucial for rest or shelter on extended adventures.

Q: Are the running trails in Cillorigo De Liébana suitable for dogs?
A: While the region is very natural and offers varied terrain, specific regulations for dogs can vary, especially within the Picos de Europa National Park. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always check local signage for specific rules regarding pets on trails.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the charming villages that serve as trailheads, such as Tama, Ojedo, and Sotres. Many routes start directly from these villages, making access convenient for runners arriving by car.
tips: About 500 meters from the chestnut grove of Pendes, heading E, is the defensive enclosure of El Corral de los Moros.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 5.43 kilometers long and is classified as moderate for jogging. While individual times vary based on fitness and pace, komoot estimates an average jogging duration of around 38 minutes for this trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Corral de los Moros circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, mostly accessible, but some sections require careful footing. You'll encounter unpaved and natural paths, with parts appearing almost like cobblestones due to exposed bedrock. There are also shady slopes where moss covers rocks and tree trunks, and you'll cross a river multiple times via wooden bridges.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or historical sites along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the main highlight is the Corral de los Moros itself. This historical site was once an ancient fortified enclosure, and from its viewpoint, you can enjoy stunning panoramic views of the Deva River, the narrow Hermida gorge, and the majestic Picos de Europa. The trail also passes through lush vegetation, ancient chestnut trees, and small waterfalls.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit Corral de los Moros – circular from Cabañes?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout the year. However, it is particularly spectacular in autumn due to the vibrant changing leaf colors of the surrounding vegetation.

faqs.question: Is the Corral de los Moros – circular from Cabañes trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate' for jogging, this route requires good physical condition due to elevation changes and varied terrain. Sure-footedness is necessary in certain sections. An easier option to reach the main viewpoint exists from the Habario recreational area, which is only 900 meters from the road, making it more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous walk.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Cabañes?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided in the available information, but Cabañes serves as the starting point for this circular route. It's advisable to check local signage or community resources for designated parking areas in or near the village before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules. It's generally recommended to check with local authorities or park information centers regarding pet policies before bringing your dog on the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to jog this route?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required for the Corral de los Moros – circular from Cabañes trail in the available information. Typically, trails in this region do not require permits for general use, but it's always good practice to verify locally if you have concerns.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects with a variant of the Camino Lebaniego, a significant pilgrimage route. Additionally, official trails along this route include sections of the Senda de la Reserva del Saja (87% covered) and the Camino Lebaniego (78% covered).

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including unpaved paths, exposed bedrock, and potential mossy sections, sturdy jogging shoes with good grip are recommended. Layers of clothing are advisable due to potential temperature changes and shady areas. While not explicitly mentioned, carrying water is always a good idea for any physical activity.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near Cabañes after the jog?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not detail specific cafes or pubs in Cabañes. However, as a village, it's likely there are local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal. It's best to check local listings or inquire upon arrival.
